page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2ne.xlarge is a storage and memory-optimized virtual machine instance configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor clocked at 2.5 GHz, the system features 4 physical cores with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supported by a cache hierarchy of 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 33 MB L3 cache. It includes hardware flags such as AVX, AVX512F, AES, and FMA, running on a KVM hypervisor.

A key hardware feature of this instance is the bundled 959 GB local SSD storage, providing high-capacity local storage directly attached to the instance. This is paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. From a performance standpoint, benchmark results place the CPU and memory subsystems in the lower tiers. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU tests, along with Geekbench and Passmark CPU scores, consistently fall into the bottom 25% performance bracket. Memory bandwidth tests also show weaker performance, with large-block read-only bandwidth registering at 20.18 GB/sec, placing it in the bottom 10%.

Qualitatively, the ecs.i2ne.xlarge offers a specific cost-efficiency profile centered on resource density rather than raw computational speed. It provides a generous 8.0 GB of RAM per core alongside nearly a terabyte of local SSD storage, making it a viable option for workloads that require localized data caching and memory capacity without demanding high-frequency CPU performance. Tradeoffs include lower single-threaded compression speeds and reduced throughput for static web serving, which benchmarked at 2.52 GB/sec.

This server type is best suited for workloads such as localized database hosting, caching layers, and memory-intensive applications that benefit from direct SSD storage. It is also applicable for moderate Redis deployments, where it achieves 2.97 million SET operations per second, and lightweight data processing tasks that do not require high-performance multi-threaded compute.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2ne.xlarge is a storage and memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and a bundled 959 GB local SSD. Operating on the x86_64 architecture with a 2.5 GHz clock speed, this KVM-based instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. While the hardware configuration offers substantial local storage and memory capacity, benchmark data indicates weaker computational and memory bandwidth performance, with CPU and memory throughput scores consistently placing in the bottom 25%. Network connectivity is supported by a 2 Gbps baseline bandwidth. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high local storage density and memory capacity over raw processing power, making it suitable for localized databases, caching layers, and moderate Redis deployments.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2ne.xlarge is a storage-optimized instance with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 959 GB local SSD, designed for localized database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.i2ne.xlarge features 4 Intel vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 959 GB local SSD storage for memory-intensive and localized database workloads.
tagline: A storage and memory-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated compute cores, high-capacity local SSD storage, and balanced memory allocation.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs at 2.5 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory with 8.0 GB per core
Suited for localized databases, caching layers, and Redis deployments
